description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a large, rectangular wooden head with smooth, flat striking surfaces on both ends. The head is made from a light-colored wood, giving it a natural, unfinished appearance. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be wrapped or textured for improved grip, with a slightly lighter tone than the head. The transition between the head and handle is seamless, suggesting a solid, traditional construction. There are no visible metal components, claws, or additional features, indicating this is a classic mallet-style hammer, likely used for woodworking or striking chisels. The overall design is simple, functional, and free from any visible branding or decorative elements.
description: A classic wooden mallet with a rectangular head and a straight, textured handle, both in natural wood tones. The design is simple and functional, with no visible metal or branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, flat face of the rectangular wooden head and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the hammer head and the cylindrical handle extending downward.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the flat end of the wooden head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the narrow side of the head and the handle visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a light, possibly textured or wrapped surface for grip.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular wooden mallet head with flat striking faces on both ends, no claws or metal parts.
